event: Madonna Enthroned with the Child, Saints Francis and Dominic, and Angels
symbols: enthroned Madonna — the Theotokos as Queen of Heaven and Seat of Wisdom
gold background — the eternal, timeless realm of divine glory
Saint Francis — voluntary poverty and conformity to Christ crucified
Saint Dominic — preaching the Word and defending the faith through truth
angels flanking — the heavenly court attending the Mother of God
Christ child holding scroll or book — the Word of God incarnate
description: A gabled panel painting with gold ground showing the Virgin Mary enthroned at center in a dark blue-black mantle, holding the infant Jesus on her left arm. The child holds what appears to be a small scroll or book and makes a gesture of blessing. Two angels lean in from behind the throne. At the lower left, a smaller figure in a brown Franciscan habit (Saint Francis) stands with a halo. At the lower right, a figure in a black and white Dominican habit (Saint Dominic) stands holding a lily. The style is Byzantine-influenced with rigid formality, gold halos, and a gold background.
theological_description: Cimabue's Madonna Enthroned is one of the foundational works of Italian painting, presenting the Theotokos (God-bearer) in majestic Byzantine solemnity. Mary enthroned with the Christ child embodies the theological truth that 'the Word became flesh and dwelt among us' (John 1:14) — God entering the world through a human mother. The gold ground represents the eternal realm: 'Before the mountains were brought forth, or ever you had formed the earth and the world, from everlasting to everlasting you are God' (Psalm 90:2). Saint Francis, bearing the stigmata and wearing his simple habit, represents the imitation of Christ in radical poverty — 'though he was rich, yet for your sake he became poor' (2 Corinthians 8:9). Saint Dominic, founder of the Order of Preachers, embodies the ministry of the Word: 'How are they to hear without someone preaching? And how are they to preach unless they are sent?' (Romans 10:14-15). The two great mendicant founders flanking the throne represent the active and contemplative dimensions of the Church's response to the Incarnation — Francis living the Gospel through poverty and love, Dominic proclaiming it through word and truth.

scripture_refs.reason: The Word made flesh — the incarnate Christ held by his mother on the throne
Christ becoming poor for our sake — Saint Francis embracing poverty in imitation
The necessity of preaching — Saint Dominic as founder of the Order of Preachers
God's eternity from everlasting to everlasting — the gold ground signifying timeless divine glory
Elizabeth's cry 'the mother of my Lord' — Mary enthroned as Theotokos

_meta.title: Madonna Enthroned with the Child, St. Francis, St. Domenico and two Angels
